module_map.responsibility: Filesystem metadata collection and extraction. Reads file attributes via std::fs and platform-specific APIs.
submodules: mod.rs (Meta struct definition, from_path entry point)
name.rs (file name parsing and display options)
size.rs (file size parsing and human-readable conversion)
owner.rs (Unix uid/gid to username resolution via /etc/passwd)
filetype.rs (file type classification: dir, symlink, etc.)

module_map.responsibility: Render formatted file listings. Converts Meta arrays to terminal output via grid/tree layouts. Handles cell alignment, padding, width calculation, and colorization integration.
submodules: grid() - term_grid layout engine, one-line/multi-line modes
tree() - recursive tree display with unicode box-drawing chars
inner_display_tree() - recursive tree cell generation with depth tracking
get_padding_rules() - per-column padding based on max widths
key_types: Block enum (Name, Size, Date, Owner, Permissions, etc.)
Cell (term_grid cell with text and styling)

module_map.responsibility: Main orchestrator. Initializes color/icon/git contexts, fetches metadata, sorts, and delegates to display modes. Single-threaded entry point for analysis pipeline.
submodules: Core::new() - initializes Colors, Icons, GitTheme, sorters
Core::fetch() - collects Meta from path array
Core::sort() - applies sort rules to Meta arrays
key_types: Core (holds Flags, Icons, Colors, GitTheme, Vec<SortFn>)

module_map.responsibility: Command-line option parsing and block definitions. Defines what metadata columns to display and how.
submodules: blocks.rs (Block enum: Name, Size, Date, Owner, Permissions, Inode, Links, Git)

module_map.responsibility: Color and icon theme resolution. Loads themes from filesystem and applies to metadata elements.
submodules: color.rs (Color struct, Elem enum for colorizable elements, theme loading)
icon.rs (Icon struct, icon theme loading by file type)

module_map.responsibility: Icon lookup and caching. Maps file types/names to Unicode/emoji icon characters.
Color element enumeration and theme context. Defines colorizable parts of output (file names, sizes, dates, etc.).
key_types: Elem enum (Name, Size, Date, Owner, etc.)

module_map.responsibility: Sorting strategy application. Functions that compare Meta structs by various criteria.
Git repository status caching. Tracks file changes within repos.

data_flow.description: Calls std::fs::symlink_metadata(), extracts ownership, permissions, file type. Calls submodule constructors (Owner::from, Size::from, Date::from, etc.). Returns Meta struct with 14 fields populated.
types_produced: Meta {name, path, permissions_or_attributes, date, owner, file_type, size, symlink, indicator, inode, links, content, access_control, git_status}

data_flow.description: Iterates paths, calls Meta::from_path() for each. Optionally recurses into directories via Meta::recurse_into(). Builds Vec<Meta>. Integrates GitCache for git_status field.
Applies SortFn comparators from Core.sorters vec. Mutates Meta vec order in-place. Sort functions access Meta fields (name, size, date, owner, etc.).
During cell generation, looks up icon character for each Meta based on file type and name patterns. Icons struct contains theme-loaded mappings.
During cell generation, wraps text in ANSI color codes. Maps Elem type (e.g., Elem::Name, Elem::Size) to Color via Colors context.
For each Meta and Block (column type), extracts relevant field (e.g., Meta.size for Block::Size). Applies icon/color. Wraps in term_grid::Cell. Adds padding via get_padding_rules().
types_produced: Vec<Cell> (one per column, colored and padded)

data_flow.description: Groups cells into rows. term_grid arranges cells with padding and alignment rules. Tree mode adds unicode box-drawing chars per depth.
term_grid renders Grid to formatted String with ANSI codes, alignment, and spacing.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central rendering engine. Inbound: core.rs (calls grid/tree), main.rs (print). Outbound: meta.rs (Meta type, OwnerCache), color.rs (Colors), icon.rs (Icons), flags/ (blocks, layout), git_theme.rs (GitTheme). Every file display passes through display::grid/tree cell rendering logic.

Main orchestrator. Inbound: main.rs (creates Core, calls run()). Outbound: display.rs (delegates), meta.rs (Meta type), flags/ (Flags config), color.rs, icon.rs (theme contexts), git.rs (GitCache), sort.rs. Coordinates all major pipeline stages.

Metadata hub. Inbound: core.rs (fetch), display.rs (Meta fields), sort.rs (comparison). Outbound: 12 submodules (owner, permissions, date, size, filetype, etc.) for metadata extraction. Every file list entry derives from Meta fields. Contains most domain logic for fs attribute extraction.

change_proposal.objective: Add SHA-256 file checksum display as a new optional column
files_to_modify: src/meta/mod.rs (add checksum: Option<String> field to Meta struct)
src/meta/checksum.rs (NEW: Checksum struct with from_path() to compute SHA-256)
src/display.rs (add checksum cell rendering in get_output())
src/core.rs (optional: early return if checksum flag not set to avoid compute)
new_types: Checksum struct { hash: String, // hex digest 64 chars }
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow Size::from_metadata() pattern: create from_path(path: &Path) -> io::Result<Checksum> function. Call it from Meta::from_path() after all other metadata is extracted. Store Option<String> in Meta to defer computation until display phase if needed.
change_proposal.integration_point: 1. Meta::from_path() calls Checksum::from_path() after Line 347 (AccessControl::for_path). 2. Block::Checksum case added to display::get_output() match statement alongside Block::Size, Block::Date. 3. Checksum column width = 64 for full SHA-256 hex, or configurable truncation. 4. Thread-safety: use sha2 crate (already likely present for utilities); io::Read on file handles is blocking (no async needed for MVP). 5. Block order: optional flag --blocks +checksum or position after Size.
risks: Performance: computing SHA-256 on large files is I/O-bound and blocking. Mitigate: add --no-checksum flag default (off by default), or cache results in session. Consider parallel processing with rayon for batch operations.
File permissions: files unreadable by user will error; handle with Result<Option<String>>, display as '-' or '<permission denied>'.
Consistency with git: git shows file hashes, lsd may diverge; document that this is SHA-256, not git object hashes.
Column width: 64-char SHA-256 may overflow narrow terminals; add truncation option (--checksum-width 8 for first 8 chars) or auto-abbreviate to terminal width.
Memory: buffering entire files for hash could exceed RAM on sparse files; use streaming reads with sha2::Update.
Syscall overhead: one open/read/close per file; TOCTTOU race on symlinks possible (follow dereferencing flags carefully).

detailed_implementation_sketch.checksum_module: pub struct Checksum { pub hash: String }; impl Checksum { pub fn from_path(path: &Path) -> io::Result<Self> { let file = File::open(path)?; let mut hasher = Sha256::new(); let mut buf = [0; 8192]; loop { let n = file.read(&mut buf)?; if n == 0 { break; } hasher.update(&buf[..n]); } Ok(Checksum { hash: format!("{:x}", hasher.finalize()) }) } }
detailed_implementation_sketch.meta_integration: In Meta::from_path(), after line 347: let checksum = match flags.checksum_enabled { true => Checksum::from_path(path).ok(), false => None }; Store in Meta.checksum: Option<String>.
detailed_implementation_sketch.display_integration: In display::get_output(), add: Block::Checksum => match &meta.checksum { Some(h) => h.clone(), None => '-'.to_string() },
detailed_implementation_sketch.flags_integration: In flags/blocks.rs, add Block::Checksum to enum. In flags.rs, add checksum_enabled: bool flag parsed from --checksum CLI arg.

tool_usage.params: max_depth=2 on src/ for directory overview with LOC and function counts
on core.rs to inspect function signatures and dependencies
on display.rs to identify rendering pipeline and Cell/Grid usage
on meta/mod.rs to trace Meta struct definition and from_path signature
focus=from_path with follow_depth=2 to trace metadata collection call chain from fs APIs through Meta field constructors
focus=display with follow_depth=1 to identify core.rs display delegation to display::grid/tree
rg '^use' to map inbound/outbound imports across modules for dependency analysis
view core.rs lines 1-50 to inspect module initialization and field setup
view display.rs lines 1-100 to inspect grid/tree function signatures and parameter types
view meta/mod.rs lines 40-110 to inspect Meta struct definition and field types
